claim_status: Official release-catalog candidate only; no installed browser path, no executable hash, no local profile, no benchmark run, no competitor result, no comparison result, and no performance claim.
source_policy: Use official browser vendor release notes or release pages before any competitor comparison.

Treat release-catalog entries as candidates only until a local benchmark manifest records executable path, executable hash, channel, command line, profile, settings, update state, and security posture.
Expire comparison evidence immediately when a tested competitor channel receives a stable update or when the benchmark suite, OS image, driver, hardware, or security settings change.

benchmark_suite_notes: BrowserBench currently identifies Speedometer 3.1 as the responsiveness benchmark that supersedes Speedometer 3.0.
BrowserBench identifies JetStream 3.0 as the current JavaScript and WebAssembly benchmark for advanced workloads.
BrowserBench identifies MotionMark as the graphics benchmark for animation capacity, with current generic MotionMark page serving version 1.3.2 at retrieval time.
Scores from different benchmark suite versions, viewport classes, display refresh rates, security settings, lifecycle states, or browser channels are not interchangeable.
unsupported_behavior: No local browser executable was inspected by this manifest.
No browser command line, profile, extension state, cache state, update state, site-isolation state, sandbox state, or memory/energy-saver setting was captured.
No competitor result or ranking exists.
No Chrome-class, fastest, lower-memory, lower-energy, compatibility, security, or production claim is supported.
evidence_required: local installed browser version capture for every compared product
executable path and executable SHA-256 or documented platform-specific hash strategy
profile, extension, sync/account, cache, command-line, update, sandbox, site-isolation, lifecycle, memory-saver, energy-saver, and feature-flag disclosure
runner-generated benchmark manifest linking hardware, OS-control, resource-attribution, corpus, network-profile, and competitor-version IDs
raw artifacts, traces, failure denominator, statistical summary, and owner review before any comparison claim
